pg66-p1-s1: was that this world and its people were often quite wrong.
pg66-p1-s2:
To conclude that others were wrong was as far as most of us
ever got.
pg66-p1-s3: The usual outcome was that people continued to wrong us and we stayed sore.
pg66-p1-s4:
Sometimes it was remorse and then
we were sore at ourselves.
pg66-p1-s5: But the more we fought and tried to have our own way, the worse matters got.
pg66-p1-s6:
As in war, the
victor only seemed to win.
pg66-p1-s7: Our moments of triumph were short-lived.
pg66-p1:
was that this world and its people were often quite wrong. To conclude that others were wrong was as far as most of us
ever got. The usual outcome was that people continued to wrong us and we stayed sore. Sometimes it was remorse and then
we were sore at ourselves. But the more we fought and tried to have our own way, the worse matters got. As in war, the
victor only seemed to win. Our moments of triumph were short-lived.
pg66-p2-s1: It is plain that a life which includes deep resentment leads only to futility and unhappiness.
pg66-p2-s2:
To the precise extent
that we permit these, do we squander the hours that might have been worth while.
pg66-p2-s3:
But with the alcoholic, whose hope is
the maintenance and growth of a spiritual experience, this business of resentment is infinitely grave.
pg66-p2-s4:
We found that it
is fatal.
pg66-p2-s5: For when harboring such feelings we shut ourselves off from the sunlight of the Spirit.
pg66-p2-s6:
The insanity of
alcohol returns and we drink again.
pg66-p2-s7: And with us, to drink is to die.
pg66-p2:
It is plain that a life which includes deep resentment leads only to futility and unhappiness. To the precise extent
that we permit these, do we squander the hours that might have been worth while. But with the alcoholic, whose hope is
the maintenance and growth of a spiritual experience, this business of resentment is infinitely grave. We found that it
is fatal. For when harboring such feelings we shut ourselves off from the sunlight of the Spirit. The insanity of
alcohol returns and we drink again. And with us, to drink is to die.
pg66-p3-s1: If we were to live, we had to be free of anger.
pg66-p3-s2: The grouch and the brainstorm were not for us.
pg66-p3-s3:
They may be the dubious
luxury of normal men, but for alcoholics these things are poison.
pg66-p3:
If we were to live, we had to be free of anger. The grouch and the brainstorm were not for us. They may be the dubious
luxury of normal men, but for alcoholics these things are poison.
pg66-p4-s1: We turned back to the list, for it held the key to the future.
pg66-p4-s2:
We were prepared to look at it from an entirely different
angle.
pg66-p4-s3: We began to see that the world and its people really dominated us.
pg66-p4-s4:
In that state, the wrong-doing of others,
fancied or real, had power to actually kill.
pg66-p4-s6:
We saw that these resentments must be mastered, but
how?
pg66-p4-s7: We could not wish them away any more than alcohol.
pg66-p4:
We turned back to the list, for it held the key to the future. We were prepared to look at it from an entirely different
angle. We began to see that the world and its people really dominated us. In that state, the wrong-doing of others,
fancied or real, had power to actually kill. How could we escape? We saw that these resentments must be mastered, but
how? We could not wish them away any more than alcohol.
pg66-p5-s1: This was our course: We realized that the people who wronged us were perhaps spiritually sick.
pg66-p5: This was our course: We realized that the people who wronged us were perhaps spiritually sick.
